title: YouTube Upload Node fails due to incorrect MIME type
symptom: The YouTube Upload Node fails with a 'Media type application/mp4 is not supported' error when attempting to upload .mp4 files.
root_cause: The YouTube Upload Node is hardcoded to send 'application/mp4' as the MIME type, which is not accepted by YouTube's API. The API requires 'video/mp4', leading to a 400 Bad Request error.
fix_steps: Access your Docker container terminal using 'docker exec -it agents sh'.
Locate the YouTube node file at '/usr/lib/node_modules/n8n/node_modules/n8n-nodes-base/dist/nodes/Google/YouTube/YouTube.node.js'.
Move the file to a shared location for editing: 'mv /usr/lib/node_modules/n8n/node_modules/n8n-nodes-base/dist/nodes/Google/YouTube/YouTube.node.js /workflows/YouTube.node.js.patch'.
Edit the file to change the MIME type to 'video/mp4'.
Move the patched file back to its original location: 'mv /workflows/YouTube.node.js.patch /usr/lib/node_modules/n8n/node_modules/n8n-nodes-base/dist/nodes/Google/YouTube/YouTube.node.js'.
Restart the n8n application with 'docker-compose down && up -d'.
agent_instruction: Patch the YouTube node source code to set the correct MIME type and restart n8n.
safety_notes: Changes will be lost if the Docker container is rebuilt or replaced.
